The other day I was at a bookstore browsing the latest novels. As is my habit, I flipped through the pages of one of the books, stopped in front of one, read its contents for a few minutes, then moved on.

This book was about self-improvement and personal growth. It talked about how reading could be an effective way to develop mental toughness – that is, the ability to withstand difficulties and continue working towards your goals despite challenges.

Reading this book got me thinking. It's not uncommon for people to view reading as a leisure activity or something to do only when they have spare time. But I realized that reading can also be a skill-building process.

The idea is simple: read voraciously, regularly. Make it a habit. The more you read, the better and wider your vocabulary will become. Over time, this could lead to better decision-making skills because of increased knowledge.

In addition, reading is not just about soaking up information; it's also about absorbing stories and ideas. Reading novels allows you to experience different cultures and perspectives. This can broaden your worldview and enrich your life in countless ways.

I am eager to kickstart this habit now. I pledge to read at least 20 pages a day, starting today. I will make a list of books to read each month, which I know will help me stay motivated. 

In conclusion, reading is not just about passing time. It's an investment in oneself that opens doors to new ideas and experiences.

Let's raise our glasses (or screens) high – cheers to the power of reading!
